trivia_bullets.text: Throne of Eldraine (ELD) was released October 4, 2019 as a top-down fairy-tale and Arthurian-legend set. Charmed Sleep is an explicit Sleeping Beauty reference: an enchanted knight falls into an unbreakable sleep. Mark Rosewater's 'Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine' confirms the team built individual cards as deliberate story callbacks.
sources: Wizards.com: Odds & Ends: Throne of Eldraine 2019-10-14

trivia_bullets.text: Charmed Sleep has been reprinted twice: Adventures in the Forgotten Realms (AFR-50, 2021, art by Zoltan Boros) and Bloomburrow (BLB-388, 2024, art by Titus Lunter — same artist as the ELD original). The Bloomburrow printing signals the card has a recurring design function across fairy-tale-coded sets.
sources: Scryfall all-printings search for Charmed Sleep

trivia_bullets.text: Charmed Sleep costs {1}{U}{U} and functions as a permanent tap lock. EDHREC tracks it in approximately 3,257 decks (0.10% of all), with the highest inclusion in Hylda of the Icy Crown builds (1.82%) and Bria, Riptide Rogue (3.16%) — commanders that care about tapping.

Art by Titus Lunter, who also illustrated the 2024 Bloomburrow reprint of the same card. The ELD composition shows a sleeping knight astride a horse beneath a rainbow — a palette choice consistent with Lunter's bright-chromatic approach in the ELD set.
No flavor text appears on this printing; the card carries rules text only.
